doc: Declarative spec. Replace <placeholders> and RUN per account, then aggregate. Uses GAQL via report.query (call shape + customer_id/login_customer_id gotcha: ../../inventory/references/tools.md). Goal = the two creative money-losers only: broken URLs + disapproved ads.

accounts.use: loop per account; keep source_user_id, company_id, id (split '-' -> customer_id, login_customer_id)

params.query: SELECT campaign.name, ad_group.name, ad_group_ad.ad.id, ad_group_ad.ad.type, ad_group_ad.ad.final_urls, ad_group_ad.policy_summary.approval_status FROM ad_group_ad WHERE ad_group_ad.status = 'ENABLED'

ads_pull.why: every enabled ad + final URLs + approval status (no metrics filter -> catches latent broken URLs)

url_check.cmd: for each UNIQUE final_url: curl -sSL -o /dev/null --max-time 25 -w '%{http_code}' -A 'creative-health-bot' "<url>"  -> write url_health.json = {url: code} (0 = no response)

params.query: SELECT ad_group_ad.ad.id, metrics.cost_micros FROM ad_group_ad WHERE ad_group_ad.status = 'ENABLED' AND segments.date DURING LAST_30_DAYS

cost_pull_optional.why: OPTIONAL: mark broken/disapproved ads that are actively spending (urgency)
cost_pull_optional.note: convert ad_id -> cost (micros/1e6) into cost.json before process.py

process: build manifest.json {accounts:[{account, account_id, ads_file, url_health_file, cost_file?}]} then: process.py manifest.json > findings.json
llm_step: the model ONLY writes the 3-line synthesis (lead with the broken+spending fix). It does NOT re-scan.
